@esbuild/linux-x64.note: Most common CI/scanner host platform. Even when this is the installed variant, all other variants MUST also be reported.

@esbuild/darwin-arm64.note: macOS Apple Silicon. Will NOT be installed on Linux/Windows scan hosts.

@esbuild/darwin-x64.note: macOS Intel. Will NOT be installed on Linux/Windows scan hosts.

@esbuild/win32-x64.note: Windows amd64. Will NOT be installed on Linux/macOS scan hosts.

known_failure_modes: Mend reports only the scan-host-matching variant (e.g. @esbuild/linux-x64 on a Linux runner) and silently drops the other three — the most common failure mode for platform-conditional optional deps.
Mend drops ALL four platform variants because optional deps whose os/cpu do not match the scan host are treated as 'not installed' and excluded from the tree.

Mend reports platform variants without optional: true — the optional flag is dropped because the resolver copies the lockfile 'packages' entry without reading the manifest's optionalDependencies field.
Mend treats @esbuild/darwin-arm64 etc. as scoped packages and strips the @esbuild/ prefix, producing artifactId 'darwin-arm64' instead of '@esbuild/darwin-arm64'.
Mend's npm-fallback JSONC parser fails to read bun.lock (comments or trailing commas trigger a parse error), producing an empty tree.

warnings: CRITICAL: ALL four platform variants (@esbuild/linux-x64, @esbuild/darwin-arm64, @esbuild/darwin-x64, @esbuild/win32-x64) MUST appear in Mend's output tree regardless of which OS the scanner runs on. If only the scan-host-matching variant is reported, Mend has dropped the others — this is a false negative. The lockfile records all variants statically; a lockfile-driven parser must emit all of them.
Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. Mend will attempt npm-resolver fallback against bun.lock. JSONC comments and trailing commas in bun.lock may cause parse failures depending on Mend UA version.

Each platform variant carries os/cpu metadata in the bun.lock entry. These fields are preserved in source_detail.marker in this expected tree. If Mend's output omits these fields, that is a metadata-loss failure (lower severity than missing the dep entirely).
The optional: true flag on each platform variant is the primary correctness signal for the optional-platform-deps pattern. If a variant appears in the tree WITHOUT optional: true, the pattern fails.
esbuild@0.19.12 itself (group: main, optional: false) is the non-optional direct dep that drives the platform-binary lookup. It must appear in the tree separately from its optional platform children.
